addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- bifrost-platform: Use this skill when an agent needs to deploy applications, manage infrastructure, authenticate, or troubleshoot delivery workflows through the Bifrost CLI. It teaches the agent to use the native `bifrost` binary directly, run a strong preflight for repo/app/platform context, prefer `git` before `gh`, pick sensible project/service defaults, and finish with deployment URLs and actionable diagnostics.
- implementation-planning: Produce an implementation plan so complete and unambiguous that a low- or medium-effort agent can build it mechanically, without re-deriving decisions. Use this whenever the user is planning, designing, scoping, or brainstorming a coding task and intends to implement it later — especially if they say things like "plan this", "design the approach", "before we code", "lay out the steps", or mention switching to a cheaper/faster model for the build. Trigger even when the user just describes a feature and asks how to approach it. The goal is a hand-off artifact: front-load all the reasoning here so implementation is translation, not invention.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	# auxilio-extension
2	
3	Google **Chrome extension (MV3)** for Auxilio (Office Ops) — the **desktop** surface for registering office visitors directly inside Google Calendar. It is the richer counterpart to the `auxilio-workspace` Calendar add-on: where the add-on is mobile-capable but lives under the CardService "card ceiling," the extension gives a full React UI (realtime guest updates, per-guest invite toggle, template preview, inline edits, intentional auto-nudge).
4	
5	> Read the umbrella `../CLAUDE.md` for the four constraints and UX rules — they apply here.
6	> Strategy + approval analysis: `../Planning-docs/2026-06-18_calendar_visitor_chrome_extension_decision.md`.
7	> Build plan: the approved plan that scaffolded this repo.
8	
9	## What this is (and isn't)
10	
11	- **Is:** an MV3 extension (WXT + React 19 + TS) that injects a trigger into Google Calendar (web) and renders the registration flow in the **side panel**. A **channel surface / dumb adapter** — it forwards intent to `office-ops-engine`.
12	- **Isn't:** a place for business logic. Dedup, draft curation, invite/pass issuance, and lifecycle all live in `office-ops-engine`'s visitor plugin. This repo renders UI and calls the engine.
13	- **Isn't:** mobile. Extensions are desktop-Chrome only. The `auxilio-workspace` add-on remains the mobile surface. Both key off the same **magic address**.
14	
15	## Architecture — three MV3 surfaces
16	
17	- **`entrypoints/background.ts`** (service worker) — the only holder of tokens + network. Mints/caches the Google `id_token` (engine auth) + `access_token` (Calendar read), runs the Calendar API client and the engine `/addon/*` client, keeps per-tab event context, routes messages.
18	- **`entrypoints/calendar.content.ts`** (content script @ `calendar.google.com`) — a **dumb trigger/observer**: detect open event, decode `eid`, inject the "Register a visitor" button (Phase 1) and the magic-address banner (Phase 2). No tokens, no business logic.
19	- **`entrypoints/sidepanel/`** (the app) — React + TanStack Query UI. Pure presentation + server state.
20	
21	```
22	content script ──msg──> background ──HTTPS(id_token)──> office-ops-engine /api/visitor/calendar/addon/*
23	side panel    ──msg──> background ──Calendar API(access_token)──> canonical iCalUID + attendees
24	```
25	
26	## Hard rules
27	
28	1. **Calendar-only scopes. Never add Gmail/Drive.** Calendar is *sensitive* (verification, no paid audit). Gmail/Drive are *restricted* → annual CASA assessment. Hard line, same as the add-on.
29	2. **Engine owns the contract.** The extension calls the existing `/api/visitor/calendar/addon/{draft,preview,send,cancel-guest}` routes (ID-token authed). Do **not** add engine signature/scope changes from here. The only engine-side change is env: add this extension's OAuth client id to `AUXILIO_WORKSPACE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` (audience allow-list).
30	3. **Background is the only secret/network holder.** Content script stays a dumb trigger; side panel stays presentation. Keep the thin-adapter boundary.
31	4. **Send the canonical `iCalUID`** (from the Calendar API `events.get`, not synthesized) so the extension path converges on the same `calendar_event_links` row as `.ics`/OAuth/add-on.
32	5. **Design: Material 3 tokens, not its code.** Our own `design/tokens.ts` + `global.css` (primary `#92288E`). No MUI/Tailwind/paper. **Icons: lucide-react only**, no Unicode glyphs/emoji in chrome.
33	6. **Animations subtle, only where needed** — CSS transitions driven by motion tokens; respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.
34	7. **Internal-first.** Force-installed/unlisted to our Workspace → no OAuth verification yet. Public/commercial = a later packaging + verification exercise (CWS review + OAuth verification), per the decision doc.
35	
36	## Stack & commands
37

# auxilio-extension

Google **Chrome extension (MV3)** for Auxilio (Office Ops) — the **desktop** surface for registering office visitors directly inside Google Calendar. It is the richer counterpart to the `auxilio-workspace` Calendar add-on: where the add-on is mobile-capable but lives under the CardService "card ceiling," the extension gives a full React UI (realtime guest updates, per-guest invite toggle, template preview, inline edits, intentional auto-nudge).

> Read the umbrella `../CLAUDE.md` for the four constraints and UX rules — they apply here.
> Strategy + approval analysis: `../Planning-docs/2026-06-18_calendar_visitor_chrome_extension_decision.md`.
> Build plan: the approved plan that scaffolded this repo.

## What this is (and isn't)

- **Is:** an MV3 extension (WXT + React 19 + TS) that injects a trigger into Google Calendar (web) and renders the registration flow in the **side panel**. A **channel surface / dumb adapter** — it forwards intent to `office-ops-engine`.
- **Isn't:** a place for business logic. Dedup, draft curation, invite/pass issuance, and lifecycle all live in `office-ops-engine`'s visitor plugin. This repo renders UI and calls the engine.
- **Isn't:** mobile. Extensions are desktop-Chrome only. The `auxilio-workspace` add-on remains the mobile surface. Both key off the same **magic address**.

## Architecture — three MV3 surfaces

- **`entrypoints/background.ts`** (service worker) — the only holder of tokens + network. Mints/caches the Google `id_token` (engine auth) + `access_token` (Calendar read), runs the Calendar API client and the engine `/addon/*` client, keeps per-tab event context, routes messages.
- **`entrypoints/calendar.content.ts`** (content script @ `calendar.google.com`) — a **dumb trigger/observer**: detect open event, decode `eid`, inject the "Register a visitor" button (Phase 1) and the magic-address banner (Phase 2). No tokens, no business logic.
- **`entrypoints/sidepanel/`** (the app) — React + TanStack Query UI. Pure presentation + server state.

```
content script ──msg──> background ──HTTPS(id_token)──> office-ops-engine /api/visitor/calendar/addon/*
side panel    ──msg──> background ──Calendar API(access_token)──> canonical iCalUID + attendees
```

## Hard rules

1. **Calendar-only scopes. Never add Gmail/Drive.** Calendar is *sensitive* (verification, no paid audit). Gmail/Drive are *restricted* → annual CASA assessment. Hard line, same as the add-on.
2. **Engine owns the contract.** The extension calls the existing `/api/visitor/calendar/addon/{draft,preview,send,cancel-guest}` routes (ID-token authed). Do **not** add engine signature/scope changes from here. The only engine-side change is env: add this extension's OAuth client id to `AUXILIO_WORKSPACE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID` (audience allow-list).
3. **Background is the only secret/network holder.** Content script stays a dumb trigger; side panel stays presentation. Keep the thin-adapter boundary.
4. **Send the canonical `iCalUID`** (from the Calendar API `events.get`, not synthesized) so the extension path converges on the same `calendar_event_links` row as `.ics`/OAuth/add-on.
5. **Design: Material 3 tokens, not its code.** Our own `design/tokens.ts` + `global.css` (primary `#92288E`). No MUI/Tailwind/paper. **Icons: lucide-react only**, no Unicode glyphs/emoji in chrome.
6. **Animations subtle, only where needed** — CSS transitions driven by motion tokens; respect `prefers-reduced-motion`.
7. **Internal-first.** Force-installed/unlisted to our Workspace → no OAuth verification yet. Public/commercial = a later packaging + verification exercise (CWS review + OAuth verification), per the decision doc.

## Stack & commands

WXT (MV3 over Vite) + React 19 + TypeScript + TanStack Query (server state) + Zustand (UI/session/event-context) + lucide-react.

```bash
npm install
npm run dev        # WXT dev server; load .output/chrome-mv3 in chrome://extensions (Developer mode → Load unpacked)
npm run build      # production build → .output/chrome-mv3
npm run compile    # tsc --noEmit (type-check)
npm run zip        # packaged zip for store upload
```

Env: copy `.env.example` → `.env` (engine URL, OAuth client id, magic address). `WXT_` vars are inlined at build.

## Versioning

**Bump `package.json` `version` on every change you ship to the browser.** WXT syncs it into `manifest.json` automatically, so `chrome://extensions` shows the loaded build's version. This is the check that a reload actually picked up your change — if the card's version doesn't match what you just built, the old bundle is still loaded (reload there and reopen the side panel). Use semver: patch for fixes, minor for features.

## Deploy / distribution

**Not Bifrost, not EAS.** Internal-first via Chrome admin **force-install** (unlisted, by extension id) or "Load unpacked" for dev. Public later via the Chrome Web Store (payments handled by our own engine; CWS no longer bills).

content.text:
All four changes are in and the type-check is clean. Here's what I did and why.

## 1 & 2 — Button on the wrong dialog / "shows up then disappears"

Both have the same root cause in `entrypoints/calendar.content.ts`. The content script injected "Manage Visitors" onto **any** open `[role="dialog"]`. Google's *"Would you like to send update emails to existing Google Calendar guests?"* confirmation (and "Delete this event?", etc.) is also a `[role="dialog"]`, so:

- it got our button injected onto it (your screenshot — issue 3), and
- when that transient dialog popped over an event and then closed, the surface changed underneath us, so the button appeared and then vanished (issue 2's flicker).

Fix: `readSurface()` now bails out whenever the open dialog isn't a real event surface, via a new `isEventDialog()` gate that only accepts a dialog showing **positive, stable event signals** — an event chip id (`[data-eventid]`), the magic address in its text, or several icon-led section rows (time/guests/location). A confirmation dialog is just a question + action buttons, so it matches none and is skipped entirely.

⚠️ One honesty caveat: I can't drive Chrome against live `calendar.google.com` from here, so I verified the logic and types but not the live DOM. The confirmation-dialog case (issue 3) is definitively fixed. The plain-event flicker (issue 2) should be fixed by the same gate *if* it was caused by the confirmation dialog hopping in — please reload the unpacked build and confirm. If the button still flickers on a normal event detail with **no** confirmation dialog involved, that's a different re-render race and I'll need the exact repro.

## 3 — "Pass sent" pill

In `RosterRow`, the name span and the pill share a flex row, but the name span wasn't allowed to shrink and the chip had no shrink protection — so a long name/email squashed or wrapped the "Pass sent" pill. Fixed in `global.css`: the name now ellipsizes (`flex: 0 1 auto; min-width: 0`) and chips never shrink (`flex: 0 0 auto`).

If the pill bug you meant was something else (wrong color/state, showing at the wrong time), tell me the symptom — this was the most likely layout cause.

## 4 — Separate guest cards

Guests were rows in one shared `Card` divided by hairlines. Now each guest is its own card with a gap between them:
- `global.css`: added `.roster` (grid + gap), made `.guest` a self-contained card (surface, border, radius, `overflow:hidden`), removed the `.guest + .guest` divider. The detail drawer's full-bleed negative margins still line up (card padding is `--space-md`/`--space-lg`, matching the drawer's offsets) and clip to the rounded corners.
- `VisitPanel.tsx`: roster now renders into `<div className="roster">` instead of the wrapping `Card`.

Version bumped `0.4.0 → 0.4.1` so `chrome://extensions` confirms the reload picked up the change. Reload the unpacked build (`npm run dev` or `npm run build` → reload) and reopen the side panel to test.

content.content:
# Auxilio Workspace

Google **Workspace Add-on** for Auxilio (Office Ops). Lets a host register an in-person visit **from inside the Google Calendar event** — the Google-sanctioned, mobile-capable version of the "Visit tab" idea. This is a **channel surface**, not a backend: it renders cards and forwards intent to `office-ops-engine`.

> Read the umbrella `../CLAUDE.md` for the four constraints and UX rules. They apply here too.

## What this is (and isn't)

- **Is:** an Apps Script add-on (CardService) in the Calendar right rail on **web + the mobile Calendar app**. **The whole host flow lives in the card** (card-first, D5) — roster, per-guest include toggle, name/phone edits, template + location pickers, email preview, send, manage — via card form inputs + action handlers that call the engine over HTTPS.
- **Isn't:** a place for business logic. Dedup, extraction, invite/pass issuance, and lifecycle flows all live in `office-ops-engine`'s visitor plugin. This repo is a **dumb adapter** (per the umbrella architecture).
- **Isn't (v1):** a web app. We **do not** bounce the host to a browser tab — that breaks "everything in front of the user," worst on mobile. A full web editor is **deferred/optional** (D6), built only on evidence the card ceiling is real.

## Hard rules (decided 2026-06-16)

1. **Contract lives in the engine, not here.** The add-on POSTs to `POST /api/visitor/calendar/addon/register` with a Google ID token; the engine verifies it, normalizes to a `NormalizedCalendarEvent{ source: 'marketplace' }`, and feeds the existing spine `ingestCalendarEvent` — the same one the `.ics`/magic-address ingest calls. Dedup is on `tenantId + iCalUID` (the `calendar_event_links` ledger), so the add-on path and the room-resource path converge instead of duplicating. **Send the canonical `iCalUID`** (from the Advanced Calendar Service `Calendar.Events.get(...).iCalUID`) so convergence actually works. Full contract: `../office-ops-engine/spec/2026-06-16_calendar_addon_register_endpoint.md`.
2. **Internal-first.** Published privately to the Appointy Workspace domain (admin one-click push to all users — no per-user install). **No Google OAuth verification needed while internal.** Public Marketplace listing is deferred packaging over this same code.
3. **Two-way without broad OAuth.** Use the narrow current-event scopes:
   - `addOns.calendar.currentEventAccess = READ_WRITE`
   - `.../auth/calendar.addons.current.event.read`, `.../auth/calendar.addons.current.event.write`

   Attendees arrive in the trigger payload (no Calendar API call). `READ_WRITE` lets us write the pass link / "✓ Registered" back onto the *open* event. Broad calendar OAuth stays in the engine's ingest pipeline — **do not duplicate it here.**
4. **Calendar-only scopes. Never add Gmail or Drive.** Calendar is a *sensitive* scope (verification but no paid audit). Gmail/Drive are *restricted* → would trigger the annual CASA security assessment. This is a hard line.

## Triggers (manifest)

- `calendar.eventOpenTrigger` → first render of the visitor card (attendees from payload, no backend call).
- `calendar.eventUpdateTrigger` → re-render on attendee add/remove; surface the per-guest include toggle (the online/offline solution).
- action handlers (edit/send) → `UrlFetchApp` POST to `POST /api/visitor/calendar/addon/*` → re-rendered card; then write confirmation back to the event.
- `calendar.homepageTrigger` (Phase 3, optional) → standalone "today's visitors" panel.

## Stack & deploy

- **Runtime:** Google Apps Script (CardService). Google-hosted — **₹0 infra.**
- **Deploy:** `clasp` (Google Apps Script CLI) → Google. **Not Bifrost, not EAS.** The umbrella's "push to `personal` remote for Bifrost deploys" rule does **not** apply here.
- **Auth to engine:** the action handler sends the host's identity (ID token); the engine verifies it so a host can only register visits for their own tenant.

## Status

**Phase 1 MVP card exists** (card-first): `appsscript.json` (manifest) + `Code.js` (CardService card — roster with include toggles + phone, preview, save & send; calls the engine with `getIdentityToken()`). Engine Phase 0 is implemented + tested. To install/test locally see **`SETUP.md`** (ngrok tunnel → local engine, real OAuth token).

Canonical plan: `../Planning-docs/2026-06-16_calendar_visitor_addon_build_plan.md`. Engine contract: `../office-ops-engine/spec/2026-06-16_calendar_addon_register_endpoint.md`. First version of `Code.js` was written outside Google — refine in the Apps Script editor (it can run/log there).

aiTitle: Fix pass sent pill and manage visitors button in auxilio-extension

content.text:
Here's what I found in the add-on's "no changes" flow and how I'd mirror it. Below is my plan — **I haven't changed anything yet.**

## 1. Remove "Where visitors check in" input
In `features/visit/VisitPanel.tsx`:
- Delete the `<TextField label="Where visitors check in" …>` block in the event summary card.
- Remove the now-dead `location` `useState`, the `locValue` calc, and the `onBlur` `patch.mutate({ location })`.
- Drop the unused `TextField` import (it's only used here in this file).

Note: the extension will simply stop setting `location`. The engine still accepts it and the **add-on keeps its location picker** — so the two surfaces diverge slightly. Flagging in case you want it gone from the add-on too (separate change).

## 2. Remove "Following the event open in Calendar"
In `VisitPanel.tsx`, delete that `<Eye/> Following the event open in Calendar` row at the top of the body. (`Eye` stays imported — the footer button still uses it.)

## 3. "No changes / up to date" message (mirroring the add-on)
The add-on computes the delta from `created`/`cancelled` and shows **"No changes."** when both are zero (`Code.js:826`); when already issued it reframes the card as **"Manage passes"** instead of "Visitor passes". The extension currently never says this — an unchanged, already-sent event still shows a prominent "Review & update passes (N)" and reports "Passes updated" even when nothing happened.

I'd add the same idea client-side:
- Compute the pending delta from the roster:
  - `pendingNew` = guests with `include && status !== 'sent' && status !== 'cancelled'`
  - `pendingCancel` = guests with `status === 'sent' && !include`
  - `hasPendingChanges = pendingNew.length > 0 || pendingCancel.length > 0`
- **Proactive:** when `data.materialized && !hasPendingChanges`, show an info banner near the action bar — e.g. *"All passes are up to date — nothing new to send."* (using the existing `.banner--info` style + a `CheckCircle2` icon).
- **Post-send:** in `SendSummary`, when `created.length === 0 && cancelled.length === 0`, show **"No changes"** instead of the current "Passes updated" (matches the add-on wording exactly).

## One decision for you
For the up-to-date state, do you want the primary button:
- **(A) Stay enabled** (my recommendation) — matches the add-on, which always allows "Review & update" so you can still push a name/phone edit onto an already-issued pass; the banner just tells the user there's nothing pending. Or
- **(B) Disabled** when there are no pending changes — stronger "nothing to do" signal, but then a pure name/phone edit on a sent guest can't be re-sent.

I lean (A). Want me to proceed with (A), or pick (B)?

Confirmed on both counts. Here's what the code actually says:

**The add-on never shows or sets location.** Zero `location` references in `Code.js`. The CLAUDE.md "location picker" note was aspirational/stale — disregard it.

**`location` is not required, and nothing consumes it.** Two different things share the name, don't confuse them:

- `visitor_locations` table / `location_id` — the real, used concept: physical check-in sites with QR codes (reception displays, check-in log sheets, etc.). **Not** what the extension input writes to.
- The draft's free-text `location` — what the "Where visitors check in" input sets. Tracing it end to end:
  - **Not required:** route coerces `body.location ? … : undefined` (`routes.ts:740`), column is nullable `location TEXT`, stored as `?? null`.
  - **Write-only / dead:** it's persisted in `visit_drafts.location` and read back into the draft response (`visit-drafts.ts:156, 216, 228, 677`) — and that's it. `applyDraft`, `materializeDraft`, invitations, passes, email rendering, and check-in **never read it**. It's not threaded into the pass or the visitor email anywhere.

So removing the input is functionally a no-op for the backend — we just stop writing a field nobody reads. I'll leave the column/types in place (harmless) and only remove the UI + the `patch.mutate({ location })` call.

That actually **simplifies my plan** — the location removal is pure dead-UI cleanup with no divergence risk from the add-on (the add-on doesn't have it either).

## What changed (all in `features/visit/VisitPanel.tsx`)

1. **Removed "Where visitors check in"** — input, its `location`/`locValue` state, the `patch.mutate({ location })` call, and the now-unused `TextField` import. Confirmed safe: the backend never reads the draft's free-text `location` (write-only/dead field), and the add-on doesn't have it either.

3. **"No changes" handling** — the update button is now disabled when there's literally nothing to act on, but re-enables on any real change:
   - **Structural changes** (detected from the roster): a new/uninvited guest still to get a pass (`pendingNew`), or a sent guest toggled off (`pendingCancel`).
   - **Invisible edits** (name/phone/template — no roster delta): tracked via an `edited` flag set in `patchGuest`/`setEventTemplate`, reset when the event changes or a send succeeds.
   - Logic: `canSend = materialized ? hasChanges : included.length > 0`. First-send is unaffected (always allowed with included guests); only already-issued events get gated.
   - When nothing's pending, the footer shows an info banner: *"All passes are up to date — nothing to send. Add a guest, toggle someone, or edit a name to send an update."*
   - Post-send wording now matches the add-on: a no-op send reads **"No changes"** instead of "Passes updated".

Reload the unpacked build at `chrome://extensions` and reopen the side panel to test. One thing worth a quick manual check: open an already-sent event with no changes (button should be disabled + banner shown), then edit a name or toggle a guest (button should re-enable).

input.content:
---
name: feedback_no_dashes_in_user_copy
description: No dashes in user-facing copy across auxilio-extension and add-on; plain simple English.
metadata:
  type: feedback
---

User-facing messages/info in `auxilio-extension` and `auxilio-workspace` (add-on) must NOT use em dashes (—), en dashes (–), or hyphen-as-connector (" - "). Write simple English statements with simple grammar and ordinary punctuation (periods, commas). Split clauses into short sentences instead of joining with a dash. Example: "Up to date — no changes to send." became "Up to date. No changes to send." Time ranges use "to" not an en dash ("9:00 to 10:00").

**Why:** Staff are not power users; the brand voice is plain and unambiguous. Typographic dashes also render inconsistently.

**How to apply:** Applies to rendered text, notifications, card labels, error/notify strings — not code comments. Hyphens inside normal compound words (check-in, sign-in, re-send) are fine. Curly apostrophes were left as-is this round; ask before mass-normalizing them. Relates to [[project_field_app_i18n]].

Done. Removed dashes from all user-facing strings in both the extension and the add-on, rewritten as plain sentences:

**auxilio-extension** (built, v0.4.4):
- `Up to date — no changes to send.` → **`Up to date. No changes to send.`**
- `Will not receive a pass — toggle on to invite` → **`Will not receive a pass. Toggle on to invite.`**
- `…no calendar UID yet — usually because it isn't…` → **`…no calendar UID yet. This usually means it is not fully saved…`**
- Time range `9:00–10:00` → **`9:00 to 10:00`**
- Notification `{title} — open Auxilio…` → **`{title}. Open Auxilio to register passes.`**
- Options page: `…live in the engine — this extension…` → **`…live in the engine. This extension…`**

**auxilio-workspace add-on** (`Code.js`):
- `Cancelled — toggle on to resend` → **`Cancelled. Toggle on to resend.`**
- `No event id in payload (…) — save the event…` → **`(…). Save the event, then reopen.`**
- `…updates passes automatically — even when this panel is closed.` → **`…automatically, even when this panel is closed.`**
- `Can't reach the engine — passes were not sent…` → **`Cannot reach the engine. Passes were not sent…`**
- `Could not switch the template — try again…` → **`Could not switch the template. Try again…`**

Type-check clean, extension rebuilt to `.output/chrome-mv3` at **0.4.4** — reload to pick it up.
